skill.name: self-improvement
skill.description: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.
skill.summary: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to markdown files for continuous improvement across AI agent sessions.
skill.author: peterskoett

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 440 potential issues, but evaluation reveals these are predominantly false positives from markdown documentation and code examples within the skill. The skill is a legitimate self-improvement/learning capture tool that creates local markdown files. No actual malicious code execution, credential exposure, or data exfiltration was found. All flagged 'external_commands' are backticks used for inline code formatting in documentation. The skill explicitly warns against logging secrets and contains no cryptographic vulnerabilities.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Access for Learning Logs
medium_findings.description: The skill creates and writes to .learnings/ directory in the project or workspace root. This is intentional functionality for capturing learnings, errors, and feature requests to local markdown files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Filesystem access is explicitly documented in SKILL.md lines 13-19. The skill creates .learnings/ directory and LEARNINGS.md, ERRORS.md, FEATURE_REQUESTS.md files. This is expected behavior for a learning capture skill.

low_findings.title: Hook Integration Scripts
low_findings.description: The skill includes optional hook scripts for Claude Code/Codex integration. These are opt-in and only output reminder text; they do not execute commands or modify files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hook scripts are explicitly opt-in per SKILL.md. The activator.sh and error-detector.sh are designed to output text reminders only, not perform file modifications.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Examples in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged 363 'external_commands' patterns in markdown files. These are backtick characters used for inline code formatting in documentation, not actual command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill is documentation that explains how to capture learnings. All shell command patterns are in markdown documentation explaining the setup process, not actual code execution paths.

content.user_title: Capture Learnings and Errors for Continuous Improvement
content.value_statement: AI coding agents often repeat the same mistakes across sessions. This skill enables Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to log learnings, errors, and corrections to local markdown files, promoting better solutions to project memory.
actual_capabilities: Log corrections and errors to .learnings/ directory with structured markdown format
Categorize entries as learnings, errors, or feature requests
Promote valuable learnings to CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project memory
Extract proven learnings into standalone skills for reuse
Integrate with Claude Code and Codex via optional hooks
Provide session reminders to review and capture learnings
limitations: Does not automatically fix errors or apply learnings
Requires manual hook setup for automatic reminders
Learnings persist locally and may need team distribution
Does not analyze or summarize entries automatically

use_cases.title: Error Knowledge Retention
use_cases.description: When Claude makes a mistake and you correct it, log the learning so future sessions avoid the same error. Example: 'Docker build fails on M1 without --platform flag'.
use_cases.target_user: Individual developers working on complex projects across multiple sessions

use_cases.title: Team Knowledge Sharing
use_cases.description: Commit learnings to the repo so all team members and agents benefit from collective experience. Useful for onboarding new team members or AI agents.
use_cases.target_user: Development teams using AI coding assistants on shared codebases

use_cases.title: Skill Extraction Pipeline
use_cases.description: When a learning pattern proves useful across multiple projects, extract it into a standalone skill. This creates reusable expertise that can be shared or published.
use_cases.target_user: Power users who want to build a personal library of AI skills from their experiences

prompt_templates.title: Log a Correction
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to log a correction. The previous approach was wrong because [reason]. The correct approach is [solution]. Please log this to .learnings/LEARNINGS.md with category 'correction'.
prompt_templates.scenario: When Claude makes a mistake and you provide the correct solution

prompt_templates.title: Log an Error
prompt_templates.prompt: A command failed with this error: [error message]. This happened when trying to [context]. Please log this to .learnings/ERRORS.md with suggested fix if identifiable.
prompt_templates.scenario: When a command or operation fails unexpectedly

prompt_templates.title: Review Learnings Before Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Before starting this task, please review .learnings/LEARNINGS.md and .learnings/ERRORS.md to see if there are relevant past learnings I should remember.
prompt_templates.scenario: At the start of a significant task to avoid repeating past mistakes

prompt_templates.title: Extract Learning to Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: This learning has proven useful multiple times. Please extract it into a standalone skill at skills/[skill-name]/SKILL.md using the self-improvement skill extraction workflow.
prompt_templates.scenario: When a pattern should become a reusable skill

output_examples.input: Log this correction: When deleting Kubernetes resources, always use kubectl delete --grace-period=0 --force to avoid hanging jobs.
output_examples.output:
Added entry to .learnings/LEARNINGS.md:

## [LRN-20260418-001] correction

**Logged**: 2026-04-18T10:30:00Z
**Priority**: high
**Status**: pending
**Area**: infra

### Summary
Always use --grace-period=0 --force with kubectl delete

### Details
Kubernetes jobs can hang indefinitely on termination. Standard kubectl delete waits for graceful termination which may never complete.

### Suggested Action
Use: kubectl delete <resource> --grace-period=0 --force

### Metadata
- Source: user_feedback
- Tags: kubernetes, cleanup, infra

output_examples.input: Review learnings before this Docker debugging task.
output_examples.output:
Relevant learnings found:

1. [LRN-20260415-003] - Docker build fails on M1 without --platform linux/amd64
2. [ERR-20260410-002] - Port 8080 already in use after container crash
3. [FEAT-20260405-001] - Need automated Docker health checks

Recommendation: Use --platform flag for builds and check port availability before starting containers.

best_practices: Log immediately after errors or corrections while context is fresh
Include specific reproduction steps and solutions, not just symptoms
Promote broadly applicable learnings to CLAUDE.md or project memory
anti_patterns: Do not log full error transcripts, stack traces, or raw command output by default
Do not commit logs containing project-specific secrets or tokens
Do not log trivial one-off mistakes that have no learning value

faq.question: Where does this skill store learnings?
faq.answer: Learnings are stored in a .learnings/ directory in your project or workspace root. It contains LEARNINGS.md for corrections and insights, ERRORS.md for command failures, and FEATURE_REQUESTS.md for requested capabilities.

faq.question: How do I enable automatic reminders?
faq.answer: For Claude Code, add hook configuration to .claude/settings.json. For OpenClaw, copy hooks/openclaw to ~/.openclaw/hooks/. The skill documentation includes step-by-step setup instructions.

faq.question: Should I commit .learnings/ to version control?
faq.answer: It depends on your team setup. Individual developers may prefer keeping learnings local. Teams benefit from shared learnings. Use .gitignore to exclude entries but keep the directory structure if you want shared templates.

faq.question: How do I promote learnings to project memory?
faq.answer: When a learning applies broadly, add it to CLAUDE.md for project facts, AGENTS.md for workflows, or TOOLS.md for tool-specific gotchas. The skill includes a promotion workflow with decision trees.

faq.question: What makes a good learning entry?
faq.answer: Good entries include: what happened, why it was wrong, what to do differently, and specific reproduction steps. Avoid vague entries like 'docker is tricky' - be specific about the problem and solution.

faq.question: How do I extract a learning into a standalone skill?
faq.answer: Run ./scripts/extract-skill.sh skill-name to create a skill directory structure. Then customize SKILL.md with the learning content. The skill documentation includes templates and quality gates for extraction.
